svg.text: 40-Light Source
I. Light Source Component Functions

The Light Source component allows Creators to mount lights on Entities. Light sources provide basic illumination and can alter the environment's lighting and atmosphere. Multiple light sources can be active within a single Light Source Component.
II. Editing the Light Source Component
Adding the Light Source Component
In the Entity/Prefab Editing interface, open the Component Editing Tab. Click Add Common Component, select Light Source, then click to add. Click Advanced Editing to expand the editing tab.
Light Source Parameters
Initially Effective: when enabled, the light source becomes effective when the entity is created.
Light Source ID: distinguishes this light source from others on the entity; cannot be edited.
Light Source Name: customizable name used for notes.
Light Source Types
Point Light: emits light in all directions from a single point.
Spotlight: emits light in a cone shape with a specific direction.
Light Parameters
Color: sets the color shown on illuminated models.
Radius: the illumination radius of the light source.
Intensity: degree of color or brightness change on the illuminated model.
Performance and Attachment
Effective Range: the light source loads only when a character enters this range (performance optimization).
Effect Range Preview: displays the active range in the Editing interface when enabled.
Attachment Point: specifies which Attachment Point the light source follows; defaults to root node.
Location and Rotation: controls position and rotation relative to the Attachment Point.
III. Node Graph Related
Toggle Entity Light Source
Enable or disable a light source on an entity at runtime through the Node Graph.
